question: Explain why increasing temperature increases the rate of reaction.
full_answer: At a higher temperature the particles have greater average kinetic energy. A greater proportion of particles has energy greater than or equal to the activation energy. Therefore the number of successful collisions per unit time increases.

rounds.prompt: At a higher temperature the particles have greater average _____ energy. A greater proportion of particles has energy greater than or equal to the activation energy. Therefore the number of successful collisions per unit time increases.

At a higher temperature the particles have greater average kinetic energy. A greater _____ of particles has energy greater than or equal to the activation energy. Therefore the number of successful collisions per unit time increases.

At a higher temperature the particles have greater average kinetic energy. A greater proportion of particles has energy greater than or equal to the _____ energy. Therefore the number of successful collisions per unit time increases.

question: State and explain the effect of a catalyst on a reaction.
full_answer: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by providing an alternative pathway with a lower activation energy. A greater proportion of collisions is therefore successful.

rounds.prompt: A catalyst increases the _____ of reaction by providing an alternative pathway with a lower activation energy. A greater proportion of collisions is therefore successful.
A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by providing an _____ pathway with a lower activation energy. A greater proportion of collisions is therefore successful.
A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by providing an alternative pathway with a lower _____ energy. A greater proportion of collisions is therefore successful.

question: State and explain the effect of using a catalyst on this reaction.
full_answer: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by providing an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy. It does not change the position of equilibrium.
